summary: Martin Fowler details the changes between the first and second editions of his seminal book Refactoring. The second edition maintains the same overall structure but updates the opening example domain, overhauls the principles and smells chapters, and rewrites the testing chapter for JavaScript instead of Java. The catalog of refactorings has been significantly revised: of the original 68, many were kept, some replaced with generalized versions (reflecting less object-centric thinking), and several dropped entirely. Fifteen completely new refactorings were added. Tangential chapters and 'big refactorings' were dropped in favor of web essays.
key_points: The book's structure remains similar but the testing chapter was completely rewritten for JavaScript
Many refactoring names were generalized to be less object-centric (e.g., 'Extract Method' became 'Extract Function')
Several first-edition refactorings were combined into single entries (Add Parameter, Remove Parameter, and Rename Method all became Change Function Declaration)
Fifteen completely new refactorings were added, including 'Replace Loop with Pipeline' and 'Split Phase'
Later chapters and 'big refactorings' were dropped because Fowler now prefers publishing such content as web essays
money_quotes: Since the first edition was so successful, I might as well continue with what worked.
Not that many people are familiar with a video store any more.
I think these kinds of topic are best captured as essays which can appear on the web.
Most are minor generalizations, such as changing 'Extract Method' to 'Extract Function'.
Many of these generalizations reflect the less object-centric nature of the rewrite.
I put together an initial chapter of refactorings that contains what I judge to be a good set of refactorings to learn about first.
key_insight: The second edition modernizes refactoring concepts by generalizing them beyond strict object-orientation and updating terminology to reflect how software development has evolved since the original 1999 publication.
